NKJV.1: And it came to pass in those days that a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered.
NKJV.2: This census first took place while Quirinius was governing Syria.
NKJV.3: So all went to be registered, everyone to his own city.
NKJV.4: Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and lineage of David,
NKJV.5: to be registered with Mary, his betrothed wife, who was with child.
NKJV.6: So it was, that while they were there, the days were completed for her to be delivered.
NKJV.7: And she brought forth her firstborn Son, and wrapped Him in swaddling cloths, and laid Him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
NKJV.8: Now there were in the same country shepherds living out in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.
NKJV.9: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were greatly afraid.
NKJV.10: Then the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid, for behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy which will be to all people.
NKJV.11: For there is born to you this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
NKJV.12: And this will be the sign to you: You will find a Babe wrapped in swaddling cloths, lying in a manger.”
NKJV.13: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying:
NKJV.14: “Glory  to God in the highest, And on earth peace, goodwill toward men!”
NKJV.15: So it was, when the angels had gone away from them into heaven, that the shepherds said to one another, “Let us now go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has come to pass, which the Lord has made known to us.”
NKJV.16: And they came with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the Babe lying in a manger.
NKJV.17: Now when they had seen Him, they made widely known the saying which was told them concerning this Child.
NKJV.18: And all those who heard it marveled at those things which were told them by the shepherds.
NKJV.19: But Mary kept all these things and pondered them in her heart.
NKJV.20: Then the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told them.
NKJV.21: And when eight days were completed for the circumcision of the Child, His name was called JESUS, the name given by the angel before He was conceived in the womb.
NKJV.22: Now when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were completed, they brought Him to Jerusalem to present Him to the Lord
NKJV.23: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every male who opens the womb shall be called holy to the LORD”),
NKJV.24: and to offer a sacrifice according to what is said in the law of the Lord, “A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”
NKJV.25: And behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon, and this man was just and devout, waiting for the Consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
NKJV.26: And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
NKJV.27: So he came by the Spirit into the temple. And when the parents brought in the Child Jesus, to do for Him according to the custom of the law,
NKJV.28: he took Him up in his arms and blessed God and said:
NKJV.29: “Lord, now You are letting Your servant depart in peace, According to Your word;
NKJV.30: For my eyes have seen Your salvation
NKJV.31: Which You have prepared before the face of all peoples,
NKJV.32: A light to bring revelation to the Gentiles, And the glory of Your people Israel.”
NKJV.33: And Joseph and His mother marveled at those things which were spoken of Him.
NKJV.34: Then Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary His mother, “Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign which will be spoken against
NKJV.35: (yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul also), that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”

NKJV.37: and this woman was a widow of about eighty-four years, who did not depart from the temple, but served God with fastings and prayers night and day.
NKJV.38: And coming in that instant she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of Him to all those who looked for redemption in Jerusalem.
NKJV.39: So when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.
NKJV.40: And the Child grew and became strong in spirit, filled with wisdom; and the grace of God was upon Him.
NKJV.41: His parents went to Jerusalem every year at the Feast of the Passover.
NKJV.42: And when He was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast.
NKJV.43: When they had finished the days, as they returned, the Boy Jesus lingered behind in Jerusalem. And Joseph and His mother did not know it;
NKJV.44: but supposing Him to have been in the company, they went a day’s journey, and sought Him among their relatives and acquaintances.
NKJV.45: So when they did not find Him, they returned to Jerusalem, seeking Him.
NKJV.46: Now so it was that after three days they found Him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, both listening to them and asking them questions.
NKJV.47: And all who heard Him were astonished at His understanding and answers.
NKJV.48: So when they saw Him, they were amazed; and His mother said to Him, “Son, why have You done this to us? Look, Your father and I have sought You anxiously.”
NKJV.49: And He said to them, “Why did you seek Me? Did you not know that I must be  about  My Father’s business?”
NKJV.50: But they did not understand the statement which He spoke to them.
NKJV.51: Then He went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was subject to them, but His mother kept all these things in her heart.
NKJV.52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

ESV.1: In those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered.
ESV.2: This was the first registration when  Quirinius was governor of Syria.
ESV.3: And all went to be registered, each to his own town.
ESV.4: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the town of Nazareth, to Judea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and lineage of David,
ESV.5: to be registered with Mary, his betrothed,  who was with child.
ESV.6: And while they were there, the time came for her to give birth.
ESV.7: And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in swaddling cloths and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.
ESV.8: And in the same region there were shepherds out in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.
ESV.9: And an angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were filled with great fear.
ESV.10: And the angel said to them, “Fear not, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.
ESV.11: For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
ESV.12: And this will be a sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in swaddling cloths and lying in a manger.”
ESV.13: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying,
ESV.14: “Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased!”
ESV.15: When the angels went away from them into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go over to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”
ESV.16: And they went with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in a manger.
ESV.17: And when they saw it, they made known the saying that had been told them concerning this child.
ESV.18: And all who heard it wondered at what the shepherds told them.
ESV.19: But Mary treasured up all these things, pondering them in her heart.
ESV.20: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, as it had been told them.
ESV.21: And at the end of eight days, when he was circumcised, he was called Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
ESV.22: And when the time came for their purification according to the Law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
ESV.23: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, “Every male who first opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”)
ESV.24: and to offer a sacrifice according to what is said in the Law of the Lord, “a pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.”
ESV.25: Now there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon, and this man was righteous and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
ESV.26: And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
ESV.27: And he came in the Spirit into the temple, and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him according to the custom of the Law,
ESV.28: he took him up in his arms and blessed God and said,
ESV.29: “Lord, now you are letting your servant  depart in peace, according to your word;
ESV.30: for my eyes have seen your salvation
ESV.31: that you have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
ESV.32: a light for revelation to the Gentiles, and for glory to your people Israel.”
ESV.33: And his father and his mother marveled at what was said about him.
ESV.34: And Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, “Behold, this child is appointed for the fall and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign that is opposed
ESV.35: (and a sword will pierce through your own soul also), so that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed.”
ESV.36: And there was a prophetess, Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years, having lived with her husband seven years from when she was a virgin,
ESV.37: and then as a widow until she was eighty-four.  She did not depart from the temple, worshiping with fasting and prayer night and day.
ESV.38: And coming up at that very hour she began to give thanks to God and to speak of him to all who were waiting for the redemption of Jerusalem.
ESV.39: And when they had performed everything according to the Law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
ESV.40: And the child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom. And the favor of God was upon him.
ESV.41: Now his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the Feast of the Passover.
ESV.42: And when he was twelve years old, they went up according to custom.
ESV.43: And when the feast was ended, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents did not know it,
ESV.44: but supposing him to be in the group they went a day's journey, but then they began to search for him among their relatives and acquaintances,
ESV.45: and when they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem, searching for him.
ESV.46: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
ESV.47: And all who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.
ESV.48: And when his parents  saw him, they were astonished. And his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us so? Behold, your father and I have been searching for you in great distress.”
ESV.49: And he said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father's house?”
ESV.50: And they did not understand the saying that he spoke to them.
ESV.51: And he went down with them and came to Nazareth and was submissive to them. And his mother treasured up all these things in her heart.
ESV.52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and in stature  and in favor with God and man.

NASB.1: Now in those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus, that a census be taken of all the inhabited earth.
NASB.2: This was the first census taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.
NASB.3: And everyone was on his way to register for the census, each to his own city.
NASB.4: Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the city of Nazareth, to Judea, to the city of David which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David,
NASB.5: in order to register along with Mary, who was engaged to him, and was with child.
NASB.6: While they were there, the days were completed for her to give birth.
NASB.7: And she gave birth to her firstborn son; and she wrapped Him in cloths, and laid Him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
NASB.8: In the same region there were [some] shepherds staying out in the fields and keeping watch over their flock by night.
NASB.9: And an angel of the Lord suddenly stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them; and they were terribly frightened.
NASB.10: But the angel said to them, 'Do not be afraid; for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be for all the people;
NASB.11: for today in the city of David there has been born for you a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
NASB.12: 'This [will be] a sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger.'
NASB.13: And suddenly there appeared with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying,
NASB.14: 'Glory to God in the highest, And on earth peace among men with whom He is pleased.'
NASB.15: When the angels had gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds [began] saying to one another, 'Let us go straight to Bethlehem then, and see this thing that has happened which the Lord has made known to us.'
NASB.16: So they came in a hurry and found their way to Mary and Joseph, and the baby as He lay in the manger.
NASB.17: When they had seen this, they made known the statement which had been told them about this Child.
NASB.18: And all who heard it wondered at the things which were told them by the shepherds.
NASB.19: But Mary treasured all these things, pondering them in her heart.
NASB.20: The shepherds went back, glorifying and praising God for all that they had heard and seen, just as had been told them.
NASB.21: And when eight days had passed, before His circumcision, His name was [then] called Jesus, the name given by the angel before He was conceived in the womb.
NASB.22: And when the days for their purification according to the law of Moses were completed, they brought Him up to Jerusalem to present Him to the Lord
NASB.23: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, 'EVERY [firstborn] MALE THAT OPENS THE WOMB SHALL BE CALLED HOLY TO THE LORD '),
NASB.24: and to offer a sacrifice according to what was said in the Law of the Lord, 'A PAIR OF TURTLEDOVES OR TWO YOUNG PIGEONS.'
NASB.25: And there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; and this man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel; and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
NASB.26: And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
NASB.27: And he came in the Spirit into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to carry out for Him the custom of the Law,
NASB.28: then he took Him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,
NASB.29: 'Now Lord, You are releasing Your bond-servant to depart in peace, According to Your word;
NASB.30: For my eyes have seen Your salvation,
NASB.31: Which You have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
NASB.32: A LIGHT OF REVELATION TO THE GENTILES, And the glory of Your people Israel.'
NASB.33: And His father and mother were amazed at the things which were being said about Him.
NASB.34: And Simeon blessed them and said to Mary His mother, 'Behold, this [Child] is appointed for the fall and rise of many in Israel, and for a sign to be opposed--
NASB.35: and a sword will pierce even your own soul-- to the end that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed.'
NASB.36: And there was a prophetess, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years and had lived with [her] husband seven years after her marriage,
NASB.37: and then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple, serving night and day with fastings and prayers.
NASB.38: At that very moment she came up and [began] giving thanks to God, and continued to speak of Him to all those who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.
NASB.39: When they had performed everything according to the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city of Nazareth.
NASB.40: The Child continued to grow and become strong, increasing in wisdom; and the grace of God was upon Him.
NASB.41: Now His parents went to Jerusalem every year at the Feast of the Passover.
NASB.42: And when He became twelve, they went up [there] according to the custom of the Feast;
NASB.43: and as they were returning, after spending the full number of days, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. But His parents were unaware of it,
NASB.44: but supposed Him to be in the caravan, and went a day's journey; and they [began] looking for Him among their relatives and acquaintances.
NASB.45: When they did not find Him, they returned to Jerusalem looking for Him.
NASB.46: Then, after three days they found Him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, both listening to them and asking them questions.
NASB.47: And all who heard Him were amazed at His understanding and His answers.
NASB.48: When they saw Him, they were astonished; and His mother said to Him, 'Son, why have You treated us this way? Behold, Your father and I have been anxiously looking for You.'
NASB.49: And He said to them, 'Why is it that you were looking for Me? Did you not know that I had to be in My Father's [house]?'
NASB.50: But they did not understand the statement which He had made to them.
NASB.51: And He went down with them and came to Nazareth, and He continued in subjection to them; and His mother treasured all [these] things in her heart.
NASB.52: And Jesus kept increasing in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

NLT.1: At that time the Roman emperor, Augustus, decreed that a census should be taken throughout the Roman Empire.
NLT.2: (This was the first census taken when Quirinius was governor of Syria.)
NLT.3: All returned to their own ancestral towns to register for this census.
NLT.4: And because Joseph was a descendant of King David, he had to go to Bethlehem in Judea, David’s ancient home. He traveled there from the village of Nazareth in Galilee.
NLT.5: He took with him Mary, to whom he was engaged, who was now expecting a child.
NLT.6: And while they were there, the time came for her baby to be born.
NLT.7: She gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him snugly in strips of cloth and laid him in a manger, because there was no lodging available for them.
NLT.8: That night there were shepherds staying in the fields nearby, guarding their flocks of sheep.
NLT.9: Suddenly, an angel of the Lord appeared among them, and the radiance of the Lord’s glory surrounded them. They were terrified,
NLT.10: but the angel reassured them. “Don’t be afraid!” he said. “I bring you good news that will bring great joy to all people.
NLT.11: The Savior — yes, the Messiah, the Lord — has been born today in Bethlehem, the city of David!
NLT.12: And you will recognize him by this sign: You will find a baby wrapped snugly in strips of cloth, lying in a manger.”
NLT.13: Suddenly, the angel was joined by a vast host of others — the armies of heaven — praising God and saying,
NLT.14:
“Glory to God in highest heaven,
and peace on earth to those with whom God is pleased.”
NLT.15: When the angels had returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let’s go to Bethlehem! Let’s see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has told us about.”
NLT.16: They hurried to the village and found Mary and Joseph. And there was the baby, lying in the manger.
NLT.17: After seeing him, the shepherds told everyone what had happened and what the angel had said to them about this child.
NLT.18: All who heard the shepherds’ story were astonished,
NLT.19: but Mary kept all these things in her heart and thought about them often.
NLT.20: The shepherds went back to their flocks, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. It was just as the angel had told them.
NLT.21: Eight days later, when the baby was circumcised, he was named Jesus, the name given him by the angel even before he was conceived.
NLT.22: Then it was time for their purification offering, as required by the law of Moses after the birth of a child; so his parents took him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord.
NLT.23: The law of the Lord says, “If a woman’s first child is a boy, he must be dedicated to the LORD.”
NLT.24: So they offered the sacrifice required in the law of the Lord — “either a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”
NLT.25: At that time there was a man in Jerusalem named Simeon. He was righteous and devout and was eagerly waiting for the Messiah to come and rescue Israel. The Holy Spirit was upon him
NLT.26: and had revealed to him that he would not die until he had seen the Lord’s Messiah.
NLT.27: That day the Spirit led him to the Temple. So when Mary and Joseph came to present the baby Jesus to the Lord as the law required,
NLT.28: Simeon was there. He took the child in his arms and praised God, saying,
NLT.29:
“Sovereign Lord, now let your servant die in peace,
as you have promised.
NLT.31: which you have prepared for all people.
NLT.32:
He is a light to reveal God to the nations,
and he is the glory of your people Israel!”
NLT.33: Jesus’ parents were amazed at what was being said about him.
NLT.34: Then Simeon blessed them, and he said to Mary, the baby’s mother, “This child is destined to cause many in Israel to fall, and many others to rise. He has been sent as a sign from God, but many will oppose him.
NLT.35: As a result, the deepest thoughts of many hearts will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your very soul.”
NLT.36: Anna, a prophet, was also there in the Temple. She was the daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher, and she was very old. Her husband died when they had been married only seven years.
NLT.37: Then she lived as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the Temple but stayed there day and night, worshiping God with fasting and prayer.
NLT.38: She came along just as Simeon was talking with Mary and Joseph, and she began praising God. She talked about the child to everyone who had been waiting expectantly for God to rescue Jerusalem.
NLT.39: When Jesus’ parents had fulfilled all the requirements of the law of the Lord, they returned home to Nazareth in Galilee.
NLT.40: There the child grew up healthy and strong. He was filled with wisdom, and God’s favor was on him.
NLT.41: Every year Jesus’ parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover festival.
NLT.42: When Jesus was twelve years old, they attended the festival as usual.
NLT.43: After the celebration was over, they started home to Nazareth, but Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didn’t miss him at first,
NLT.44: because they assumed he was among the other travelers. But when he didn’t show up that evening, they started looking for him among their relatives and friends.
NLT.45: When they couldn’t find him, they went back to Jerusalem to search for him there.
NLT.46: Three days later they finally discovered him in the Temple, sitting among the religious teachers, listening to them and asking questions.
NLT.47: All who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.
NLT.48: His parents didn’t know what to think. “Son,” his mother said to him, “why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been frantic, searching for you everywhere.”
NLT.49: “But why did you need to search?” he asked. “Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?”
NLT.50: But they didn’t understand what he meant.
NLT.51: Then he returned to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. And his mother stored all these things in her heart.
NLT.52: Jesus grew in wisdom and in stature and in favor with God and all the people.

WEB.1: Now in those days, a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be enrolled.
WEB.2: This was the first enrollment made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
WEB.3: All went to enroll themselves, everyone to his own city.
WEB.4: Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judea, to David’s city, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David;
WEB.5: to enroll himself with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him as wife, being pregnant.
WEB.6: While they were there, the day had come for her to give birth.
WEB.7: She gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a feeding trough, because there was no room for them in the inn.
WEB.8: There were shepherds in the same country staying in the field, and keeping watch by night over their flock.
WEB.9: Behold, an angel of the Lord stood by them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
WEB.10: The angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be to all the people.
WEB.11: For there is born to you today, in David’s city, a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
WEB.12: This is the sign to you: you will find a baby wrapped in strips of cloth, lying in a feeding trough.”
WEB.13: Suddenly, there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly army praising God, and saying,
WEB.14: “Glory to God in the highest, on earth peace, good will toward men.”
WEB.15: When the angels went away from them into the sky, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem, now, and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”
WEB.16: They came with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby was lying in the feeding trough.
WEB.17: When they saw it, they publicized widely the saying which was spoken to them about this child.
WEB.18: All who heard it wondered at the things which were spoken to them by the shepherds.
WEB.19: But Mary kept all these sayings, pondering them in her heart.
WEB.20: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, just as it was told them.
WEB.21: When eight days were fulfilled for the circumcision of the child, his name was called Jesus, which was given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
WEB.22: When the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were fulfilled, they brought him up to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord
WEB.23: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every male who opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”),
WEB.24: and to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, “A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.”
WEB.25: Behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.
WEB.26: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
WEB.27: He came in the Spirit into the temple. When the parents brought in the child, Jesus, that they might do concerning him according to the custom of the law,
WEB.28: then he received him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,
WEB.29: “Now you are releasing your servant, Master, according to your word, in peace;
WEB.30: for my eyes have seen your salvation,
WEB.31: which you have prepared before the face of all peoples;
WEB.32: a light for revelation to the nations, and the glory of your people Israel.”
WEB.33: Joseph and his mother were marveling at the things which were spoken concerning him,
WEB.34: and Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary, his mother, “Behold, this child is set for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and for a sign which is spoken against.
WEB.35: Yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul, that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”
WEB.36: There was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher (she was of a great age, having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,
WEB.37: and she had been a widow for about eighty-four years), who didn’t depart from the temple, worshiping with fastings and petitions night and day.
WEB.38: Coming up at that very hour, she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of him to all those who were looking for redemption in Jerusalem.
WEB.39: When they had accomplished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.
WEB.40: The child was growing, and was becoming strong in spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.
WEB.41: His parents went every year to Jerusalem at the feast of the Passover.
WEB.42: When he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast,
WEB.43: and when they had fulfilled the days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. Joseph and his mother didn’t know it,
WEB.44: but supposing him to be in the company, they went a day’s journey, and they looked for him among their relatives and acquaintances.
WEB.45: When they didn’t find him, they returned to Jerusalem, looking for him.
WEB.46: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the middle of the teachers, both listening to them, and asking them questions.
WEB.47: All who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.
WEB.48: When they saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us this way? Behold, your father and I were anxiously looking for you.”
WEB.49: He said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?”
WEB.50: They didn’t understand the saying which he spoke to them.
WEB.51: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth. He was subject to them, and his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.
WEB.52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

CSB17.2: This first registration took place while <sup>[2]</sup> Quirinius was governing Syria. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.3: So everyone went to be registered, each to his own town.
CSB17.4: Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth <sup>ⓝ</sup> in Galilee, to Judea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem, <sup>ⓐ</sup> because he was of the house and family line of David, <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.5: to be registered along with Mary, who was engaged to him <sup>[3]</sup> and was pregnant.
CSB17.6: While they were there, the time came for her to give birth.
CSB17.7: Then she gave birth to her firstborn Son, and she wrapped him tightly in cloth and laid him in a manger, <sup>[4]</sup> because there was no guest room available for them.
CSB17.8: In the same region, shepherds were staying out in the fields and keeping watch at night over their flock.
CSB17.9: Then an angel of the Lord <sup>ⓒ</sup> stood before them, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and the glory of the Lord <sup>ⓔ</sup> shone around them, and they were terrified. <sup>[5]</sup>
CSB17.10: But the angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, <sup>ⓕ</sup> for look, I proclaim to you good news of great joy that will be for all the people: <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.11: Today in the city of David a Savior <sup>ⓖ</sup> was born for you, who is the Messiah, <sup>ⓗ</sup> the Lord. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.12: This will be the sign for you: <sup>ⓙ</sup> You will find a baby wrapped tightly in cloth and lying in a manger.”
CSB17.13: Suddenly there was a multitude of the heavenly host <sup>[7]</sup> with the angel, praising God and saying:
CSB17.14:
Glory to God in the highest heaven, ⓚ
and peace on earth ⓛ to people he favors! [8] [9] ⓜ
CSB17.15: When the angels had left them and returned to heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go straight to Bethlehem and see what has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”
CSB17.16: They hurried off and found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby who was lying in the manger.
CSB17.17: After seeing them, they reported the message they were told about this child,
CSB17.18: and all who heard it were amazed <sup>ⓝ</sup> at what the shepherds said to them.
CSB17.19: But Mary was treasuring up all these things in her heart <sup>ⓐ</sup> and meditating on them.
CSB17.20: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God <sup>ⓑ</sup> for all the things they had seen and heard, which were just as they had been told.
CSB17.21: When the eight days were completed for his circumcision, <sup>ⓒ</sup> he was named Jesus <sup>ⓓ</sup>  — the name given by the angel before he was conceived.
CSB17.22: And when the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were finished, <sup>ⓔ</sup> they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
CSB17.23: (just as it is written in the law of the Lord,Every firstborn male will be dedicated [10]to the Lord [11] ⓕ)
CSB17.24: and to offer a sacrifice (according to what is stated in the law of the Lord, a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons [12] ⓖ).
CSB17.25: There was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, <sup>ⓗ</sup> looking forward to Israel’s consolation, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and the Holy Spirit was on him.
CSB17.26: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit <sup>ⓙ</sup> that he would not see death before he saw the Lord’s Messiah. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.27: Guided by the Spirit, he entered the temple. When the parents brought in the child Jesus to perform for him what was customary under the law,
CSB17.28: Simeon took him up in his arms, praised God, and said,
CSB17.29:
Now, Master,
you can dismiss your servant in peace,
as you promised.
CSB17.30: For my eyes have seen your salvation. ⓛ
CSB17.31:
You have prepared it
in the presence of all peoples —
CSB17.32:
a light for revelation to the Gentiles [13] ⓜ
and glory to your people Israel. ⓝ
CSB17.33: His father and mother <sup>[14]</sup> were amazed at what was being said about him.
CSB17.34: Then Simeon blessed them and told his mother Mary: “Indeed, this child is destined to cause the fall and rise of many in Israel <sup>ⓐ</sup> and to be a sign that will be opposed <sup>[15]</sup>  —
CSB17.35: and a sword will pierce your own soul — that the thoughts <sup>[16]</sup> of many hearts may be revealed.”
CSB17.36: There was also a prophetess, <sup>ⓑ</sup> Anna, a daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. <sup>ⓒ</sup> She was well along in years, having lived with her husband seven years after her marriage, <sup>[17]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.37: and was a widow for eighty-four years. <sup>[18]</sup> She did not leave the temple, serving God night and day with fasting and prayers. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.38: At that very moment, <sup>[19]</sup> she came up and began to thank God and to speak about him to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem. <sup>[20]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.39: When they had completed everything according to the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.40: The boy grew up and became strong, filled with wisdom, and God’s grace was on him. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.41: Every year his parents traveled to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.42: When he was twelve years old, they went up according to the custom of the festival. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.43: After those days were over, <sup>ⓚ</sup> as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents <sup>[21]</sup> did not know it.
CSB17.44: Assuming he was in the traveling party, they went a day’s journey. Then they began looking for him among their relatives and friends.
CSB17.45: When they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for him.
CSB17.46: After three days, they found him in the temple sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
CSB17.47: And all those who heard him were astounded at his understanding and his answers.
CSB17.48: When his parents saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, <sup>ⓛ</sup> “Son, why have you treated us like this? Your father and I have been anxiously searching for you.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.49: “Why were you searching for me?” he asked them. “Didn’t you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Father’s house?” <sup>[22]</sup>
CSB17.50: But they did not understand what he said to them. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.51: Then he went down with them and came to Nazareth and was obedient to them. His mother kept all these things in her heart. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and with people. <sup>ⓑ</sup>

AMP.1: Now in those days a decree went out from [the emperor] Caesar Augustus, that all the inhabited world (the Roman Empire) should be registered [in a census].
AMP.2: This was the first census taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.
AMP.3: And everyone went to register for the census, each to his own city.
AMP.4: So Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the city of Nazareth, to Judea, to the city of David which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David,
AMP.5: in order to register with Mary, who was betrothed to him, and was with child.
AMP.6: While they were there [in Bethlehem], the time came for her to give birth,
AMP.7: and she gave birth to her Son, her firstborn; and she wrapped Him in [ swaddling] cloths and laid Him in a manger, because there was no [private] room for them in the inn.
AMP.8: In the same region there were shepherds staying out in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.
AMP.9: And an angel of the Lord suddenly stood before them, and the glory of the Lord flashed and shone around them, and they were terribly frightened.
AMP.10: But the angel said to them, "Do not be afraid; for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be for all the people.
AMP.11: For this day in the city of David there has been born for you a Savior, who is Christ the Lord (the Messiah).
AMP.12: And this will be a sign for you [by which you will recognize Him]: you will find a Baby wrapped in [swaddling] cloths and lying in a manger."
AMP.13: Then suddenly there appeared with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host (angelic army) praising God and saying,
AMP.14:
"Glory to God in the highest [heaven],
And on earth peace among men with whom He is well-pleased."
AMP.15: When the angels had gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds began saying one to another, "Let us go straight to Bethlehem, and see this [wonderful] thing that has happened which the Lord has made known to us."
AMP.16: So they went in a hurry and found their way to Mary and Joseph, and the Baby as He lay in the manger.
AMP.17: And when they had seen this, they made known what had been told them about this Child,
AMP.18: and all who heard it were astounded and wondered at what the shepherds told them.
AMP.19: But Mary treasured all these things, giving careful thought to them and pondering them in her heart.
AMP.20: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all that they had heard and seen, just as it had been told them.
AMP.21: At the end of eight days, when He was to be circumcised, He was named Jesus, the name given [to Him] by the angel [Gabriel] before He was conceived in the womb.
AMP.22: And when the time for their purification came [that is, the mother's purification and the baby's dedication] according to the Law of Moses, they brought Him up to Jerusalem to present Him to the Lord [set apart as the Firstborn]
AMP.23: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, "Every firstborn male that opens the womb shall be called holy [set apart and dedicated] to the Lord)"
AMP.24: and [they came also] to offer a sacrifice according to what is said in the Law of the Lord [to be appropriate for a family of modest means], "a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons."
AMP.25: Now there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; and this man was righteous and devout [carefully observing the divine Law], and looking for the Consolation of Israel; and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
AMP.26: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not die before he had seen the Lord's Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed).
AMP.27: Prompted by the Spirit, he came into the temple [enclosure]; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for Him the custom required by the Law,
AMP.28: Simeon took Him into his arms, and blessed and praised and thanked God, and said,
AMP.29:
"Now, Lord, You are releasing Your bond-servant to leave [this world] in peace,
According to Your word;
AMP.30: For my eyes have seen Your Salvation,
AMP.31: Which You have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
AMP.32:
A Light for revelation to the Gentiles [to disclose what was previously unknown],
And [to bring] the praise and honor and glory of Your people Israel."
AMP.33: And His [legal] father and His mother were amazed at what was said about Him.
AMP.34: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary His mother, "Listen carefully: this Child is appointed and destined for the fall and rise of many in Israel, and for a sign that is to be opposed—
AMP.35: and a sword [of deep sorrow] will pierce through your own soul—so that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed."
AMP.36: There was a prophetess, Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was very old, and had lived with her husband for seven years after her marriage,
AMP.37: and then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She did not leave the [area of the] temple, but was serving and worshiping night and day with fastings and prayers.
AMP.38: She, too, came up at that very moment and began praising and giving thanks to God, and continued to speak of Him to all who were looking for the redemption and deliverance of Jerusalem.
AMP.39: And when they had done everything [in connection with Jesus' birth] according to the Law of the Lord, they went back to Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.
AMP.40: And the Child continued to grow and become strong [in spirit], filled with wisdom; and the grace (favor, spiritual blessing) of God was upon Him.
AMP.41: Now His parents went to Jerusalem every year for the Passover Feast.
AMP.42: And when He was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem, according to the custom of the Feast;
AMP.43: and as they were returning [to Nazareth], after spending the required number of days [at the Feast], the boy Jesus remained behind in Jerusalem. Now His parents did not know this,
AMP.44: but supposed Him to be in the caravan, and traveled a day's journey; and [then] they began searching [anxiously] for Him among their relatives and acquaintances.
AMP.45: When they did not find Him, they went back to Jerusalem looking for Him [everywhere].
AMP.46: Three days later they found Him in the [court of the] temple, sitting among the teachers, both listening to them and asking them questions.
AMP.47: All who heard Him were amazed by His intelligence and His understanding and His answers.
AMP.48: When they saw Him, they were overwhelmed; and His mother said to Him, "Son, why have You treated us like this? Listen, Your father and I have been [greatly distressed and] anxiously looking for You."
AMP.49: And He answered, "Why did you have to look for Me? Did you not know that I had to be in My Father's house?"
AMP.50: But they did not understand what He had said to them.
AMP.51: He went down to Nazareth with them, and was continually submissive and obedient to them; and His mother treasured all these things in her heart.
AMP.52: And Jesus kept increasing in wisdom and in stature, and in favor with God and men.

MSG.1: About that time Caesar Augustus ordered a census to be taken throughout the Empire.
MSG.2: This was the first census when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
MSG.3: Everyone had to travel to his own ancestral hometown to be accounted for.
MSG.4: So Joseph went from the Galilean town of Nazareth up to Bethlehem in Judah, David's town, for the census. As a descendant of David, he had to go there.
MSG.5: He went with Mary, his fianc-e, who was pregnant.
MSG.6: While they were there, the time came for her to give birth.
MSG.7: She gave birth to a son, her firstborn. She wrapped him in a blanket and laid him in a manger, because there was no room in the hostel.
MSG.8: There were sheepherders camping in the neighborhood. They had set night watches over their sheep.
MSG.9: Suddenly, God's angel stood among them and God's glory blazed around them. They were terrified.
MSG.10: The angel said, "Don't be afraid. I'm here to announce a great and joyful event that is meant for everybody, worldwide:
MSG.11: A Savior has just been born in David's town, a Savior who is Messiah and Master.
MSG.12: This is what you're to look for: a baby wrapped in a blanket and lying in a manger."
MSG.13: At once the angel was joined by a huge angelic choir singing God's praises:
MSG.14: Glory to God in the heavenly heights, Peace to all men and women on earth who please him.
MSG.15: As the angel choir withdrew into heaven, the sheepherders talked it over. "Let's get over to Bethlehem as fast as we can and see for ourselves what God has revealed to us."
MSG.16: They left, running, and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
MSG.17: Seeing was believing. They told everyone they met what the angels had said about this child.
MSG.18: All who heard the sheepherders were impressed.
MSG.19: Mary kept all these things to herself, holding them dear, deep within herself.
MSG.20: The sheepherders returned and let loose, glorifying and praising God for everything they had heard and seen. It turned out exactly the way they'd been told!
MSG.21: When the eighth day arrived, the day of circumcision, the child was named Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived.
MSG.22: Then when the days stipulated by Moses for purification were complete, they took him up to Jerusalem to offer him to God
MSG.23: as commanded in God's Law: "Every male who opens the womb shall be a holy offering to God,"
MSG.24: and also to sacrifice the "pair of doves or two young pigeons" prescribed in God's Law.
MSG.25: In Jerusalem at the time, there was a man, Simeon by name, a good man, a man who lived in the prayerful expectancy of help for Israel. And the Holy Spirit was on him.
MSG.26: The Holy Spirit had shown him that he would see the Messiah of God before he died.
MSG.27: Led by the Spirit, he entered the Temple. As the parents of the child Jesus brought him in to carry out the rituals of the Law,
MSG.28: Simeon took him into his arms and blessed God:
MSG.29: God, you can now release your servant; release me in peace as you promised.
MSG.30: With my own eyes I've seen your salvation;
MSG.31: it's now out in the open for everyone to see:
MSG.32: A God-revealing light to the non-Jewish nations, and of glory for your people Israel.
MSG.33: Jesus' father and mother were speechless with surprise at these words.
MSG.34: Simeon went on to bless them, and said to Mary his mother, This child marks both the failure and the recovery of many in Israel, A figure misunderstood and contradicted—
MSG.35: the pain of a sword-thrust through you— But the rejection will force honesty, as God reveals who they really are.
MSG.36: Anna the prophetess was also there, a daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher. She was by now a very old woman. She had been married seven years
MSG.37: and a widow for eighty-four. She never left the Temple area, worshiping night and day with her fastings and prayers.
MSG.38: At the very time Simeon was praying, she showed up, broke into an anthem of praise to God, and talked about the child to all who were waiting expectantly for the freeing of Jerusalem.
MSG.39: When they finished everything required by God in the Law, they returned to Galilee and their own town, Nazareth.
MSG.40: There the child grew strong in body and wise in spirit. And the grace of God was on him.
MSG.41: Every year Jesus' parents traveled to Jerusalem for the Feast of Passover.
MSG.42: When he was twelve years old, they went up as they always did for the Feast.
MSG.43: When it was over and they left for home, the child Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents didn't know it.
MSG.44: Thinking he was somewhere in the company of pilgrims, they journeyed for a whole day and then began looking for him among relatives and neighbors.
MSG.45: When they didn't find him, they went back to Jerusalem looking for him.
MSG.46: The next day they found him in the Temple seated among the teachers, listening to them and asking questions.
MSG.47: The teachers were all quite taken with him, impressed with the sharpness of his answers.
MSG.48: But his parents were not impressed; they were upset and hurt. His mother said, "Young man, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been half out of our minds looking for you."
MSG.49: He said, "Why were you looking for me? Didn't you know that I had to be here, dealing with the things of my Father?"
MSG.50: But they had no idea what he was talking about.
MSG.51: So he went back to Nazareth with them, and lived obediently with them. His mother held these things dearly, deep within herself.
MSG.52: And Jesus matured, growing up in both body and spirit, blessed by both God and people.

NIV.1:
The Birth of Jesus
In those days Caesar Augustus issued a decree that a census should be taken of the entire Roman world.
NIV.2: (This was the first census that took place while Quirinius was governor of Syria.)
NIV.3: And everyone went to his own town to register.
NIV.4: So Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to Bethlehem the town of David, because he belonged to the house and line of David.
NIV.5: He went there to register with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him and was expecting a child.
NIV.6: While they were there, the time came for the baby to be born,
NIV.7: and she gave birth to her firstborn, a son. She wrapped him in cloths and placed him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
NIV.8:
The Shepherds and the Angels
And there were shepherds living out in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night.
NIV.9: An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
NIV.10: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid. I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.
NIV.11: Today in the town of David a Savior has been born to you; he is Christ the Lord.
NIV.12: This will be a sign to you: You will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger.”
NIV.13: Suddenly a great company of the heavenly host appeared with the angel, praising God and saying,
NIV.14:
“Glory to God in the highest,
and on earth peace to men on whom his favor rests.”
NIV.15: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has told us about.”
NIV.16: So they hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby, who was lying in the manger.
NIV.17: When they had seen him, they spread the word concerning what had been told them about this child,
NIV.18: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds said to them.
NIV.19: But Mary treasured up all these things and pondered them in her heart.
NIV.20: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things they had heard and seen, which were just as they had been told.
NIV.21:
Jesus Presented in the Temple
On the eighth day, when it was time to circumcise him, he was named Jesus, the name the angel had given him before he had been conceived.
NIV.22: When the time of their purification according to the Law of Moses had been completed, Joseph and Mary took him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
NIV.23: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male is to be consecrated to the Lord”),
NIV.24: and to offer a sacrifice in keeping with what is said in the Law of the Lord: “a pair of doves or two young pigeons.”
NIV.25: Now there was a man in Jerusalem called Simeon, who was righteous and devout. He was waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
NIV.26: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not die before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
NIV.27: Moved by the Spirit, he went into the temple courts. When the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him what the custom of the Law required,
NIV.28: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying:
NIV.29:
“Sovereign Lord, as you have promised,
you now dismiss your servant in peace.
NIV.30: For my eyes have seen your salvation,
NIV.31: which you have prepared in the sight of all people,
NIV.32:
a light for revelation to the Gentiles
and for glory to your people Israel.”
NIV.33: The child’s father and mother marveled at what was said about him.
NIV.34: Then Simeon blessed them and said to Mary, his mother: “This child is destined to cause the falling and rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be spoken against,
NIV.35: so that the thoughts of many hearts will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your own soul too.”
NIV.36: There was also a prophetess, Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was very old; she had lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
NIV.37: and then was a widow until she was eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped night and day, fasting and praying.
NIV.38: Coming up to them at that very moment, she gave thanks to God and spoke about the child to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
NIV.39: When Joseph and Mary had done everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee to their own town of Nazareth.
NIV.40: And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.
NIV.41:
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
Every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Feast of the Passover.
NIV.42: When he was twelve years old, they went up to the Feast, according to the custom.
NIV.43: After the Feast was over, while his parents were returning home, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but they were unaware of it.
NIV.44: Thinking he was in their company, they traveled on for a day. Then they began looking for him among their relatives and friends.
NIV.45: When they did not find him, they went back to Jerusalem to look for him.
NIV.46: After three days they found him in the temple courts, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
NIV.47: Everyone who heard him was amazed at his understanding and his answers.
NIV.48: When his parents saw him, they were astonished. His mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us like this? Your father and I have been anxiously searching for you.”
NIV.49: “Why were you searching for me?” he asked. “Didn’t you know I had to be in my Father’s house?”
NIV.50: But they did not understand what he was saying to them.
NIV.51: Then he went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. But his mother treasured all these things in her heart.
NIV.52: And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

NRSVCE.33: And the child’s father and mother were amazed at what was being said about him.
NRSVCE.34: Then Simeon blessed them and said to his mother Mary, “This child is destined for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be opposed
NRSVCE.35: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed — and a sword will pierce your own soul too.”
NRSVCE.36: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was of a great age, having lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
NRSVCE.37: then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped there with fasting and prayer night and day.
NRSVCE.38: At that moment she came, and began to praise God and to speak about the child to all who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.
NRSVCE.39: When they had finished everything required by the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
NRSVCE.40: The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom; and the favor of God was upon him.
NRSVCE.41: Now every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the festival of the Passover.
NRSVCE.42: And when he was twelve years old, they went up as usual for the festival.
NRSVCE.43: When the festival was ended and they started to return, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents did not know it.
NRSVCE.44: Assuming that he was in the group of travelers, they went a day’s journey. Then they started to look for him among their relatives and friends.
NRSVCE.45: When they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for him.
NRSVCE.46: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
NRSVCE.47: And all who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.
NRSVCE.48: When his parents saw him they were astonished; and his mother said to him, “Child, why have you treated us like this? Look, your father and I have been searching for you in great anxiety.”
NRSVCE.49: He said to them, “Why were you searching for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father’s house?”
NRSVCE.50: But they did not understand what he said to them.
NRSVCE.51: Then he went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was obedient to them. His mother treasured all these things in her heart.
NRSVCE.52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and in years, and in divine and human favor.

NET.1: Now in those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus to register all the empire for taxes.
NET.2: This was the first registration, taken when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
NET.3: Everyone went to his own town to be registered.
NET.4: So Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family line of David.
NET.5: He went to be registered with Mary, who was promised in marriage to him, and who was expecting a child.
NET.6: While they were there, the time came for her to deliver her child.
NET.7: And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in strips of cloth and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.
NET.8: Now there were shepherds nearby living out in the field, keeping guard over their flock at night.
NET.9: An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were absolutely terrified.
NET.10: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid! Listen carefully, for I proclaim to you good news that brings great joy to all the people:
NET.11: Today your Savior is born in the city of David. He is Christ the Lord.
NET.12: This will be a sign for you: You will find a baby wrapped in strips of cloth and lying in a manger.”
NET.13: Suddenly a vast, heavenly army appeared with the angel, praising God and saying,
NET.14: “Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among people with whom he is pleased!”
NET.15: When the angels left them and went back to heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go over to Bethlehem and see this thing that has taken place, that the Lord has made known to us.”
NET.16: So they hurried off and located Mary and Joseph, and found the baby lying in a manger.
NET.17: When they saw him, they related what they had been told about this child,
NET.18: and all who heard it were astonished at what the shepherds said.
NET.19: But Mary treasured up all these words, pondering in her heart what they might mean.
NET.20: So the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen; everything was just as they had been told.
NET.21: At the end of eight days, when he was circumcised, he was named Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
NET.22: Now when the time came for their purification according to the law of Moses, Joseph and Mary brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
NET.23: (just as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male will be set apart to the Lord” ),
NET.24: and to offer a sacrifice according to what is specified in the law of the Lord, a pair of doves or two young pigeons.
NET.25: Now there was a man in Jerusalem named Simeon who was righteous and devout, looking for the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
NET.26: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not die before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
NET.27: So Simeon, directed by the Spirit, came into the temple courts, and when the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him what was customary according to the law,
NET.28: Simeon took him in his arms and blessed God, saying,
NET.29: “Now, according to your word, Sovereign Lord, permit your servant to depart in peace.
NET.30: For my eyes have seen your salvation
NET.31: that you have prepared in the presence of all peoples:
NET.32: a light, for revelation to the Gentiles, and for glory to your people Israel.”
NET.33: So the child’s father and mother were amazed at what was said about him.
NET.34: Then Simeon blessed them and said to his mother Mary, “Listen carefully: This child is destined to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that will be rejected.
NET.35: Indeed, as a result of him the thoughts of many hearts will be revealed — and a sword will pierce your own soul as well!”
NET.36: There was also a prophetess, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was very old, having been married to her husband for seven years until his death.
NET.37: She had lived as a widow since then for eighty-four years. She never left the temple, worshiping with fasting and prayer night and day.
NET.38: At that moment, she came up to them and began to give thanks to God and to speak about the child to all who were waiting for the redemption of Jerusalem.
NET.39: So when Joseph and Mary had performed everything according to the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
NET.40: And the child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom, and the favor of God was upon him.
NET.41: Now Jesus’ parents went to Jerusalem every year for the feast of the Passover.
NET.42: When he was twelve years old, they went up according to custom.
NET.43: But when the feast was over, as they were returning home, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents did not know it,
NET.44: but (because they assumed that he was in their group of travelers) they went a day’s journey. Then they began to look for him among their relatives and acquaintances.
NET.45: When they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
NET.46: After three days they found him in the temple courts, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
NET.47: And all who heard Jesus were astonished at his understanding and his answers.
NET.48: When his parents saw him, they were overwhelmed. His mother said to him, “Child, why have you treated us like this? Look, your father and I have been looking for you anxiously.”
NET.49: But he replied, “Why were you looking for me? Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?”
NET.50: Yet his parents did not understand the remark he made to them.
NET.51: Then he went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was obedient to them. But his mother kept all these things in her heart.
NET.52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and in stature, and in favor with God and with people.
